mnemonics.title: Rotation Formula Rememberer
mnemonics.content: Use 'x-cos-y-sin' for x' and 'x-sin-y-cos' for y'. Think: 'Come Sin Come' - COme SIn for COs, Come for x' and SIn for y'.

mnemonics.title: Pivot Rotation Steps
mnemonics.content: TTP - Translate to Origin, Transform (rotate), Translate back. Remember: 'Take Two Pies' = Translate Translate Pivot.

mnemonics.title: Positive Rotation Direction
mnemonics.content: CCW = CounterClockWise = CAAS = Counter Adds Angle, clock is the opposite. CCW is the standard positive direction in graphics.

mnemonics.title: Determinant of Rotation
mnemonics.content: Det(R) = 1 always - 'One' rotation preserves area. Think: 'One' means 'won't change' (won't scale).

acronyms.usage: Steps for rotation about arbitrary pivot point
Positive rotation direction in computer graphics

visualTips.visualization: Think of the 2x2 matrix as [cos -sin; sin cos]. The sin terms are on opposite diagonals. cos is always on the main diagonal.
i-hat (1,0) rotates to (cosθ, sinθ), j-hat (0,1) rotates to (-sinθ, cosθ). The matrix columns are these rotated unit vectors.
A point (x,y) in Cartesian becomes (r,θ) in polar. After rotation by α, it becomes (r, θ+α). Then convert back to Cartesian.
Draw a circle centered at pivot. Any point on the circle stays on the circle after rotation - only its angle changes.

analogies.analogy: Like a clock's hour hand rotating around the center pivot. The hand's length (distance from center) never changes.
Imagine spinning a key on a keyring - the keyring is the pivot point, and you rotate the key around that fixed point.
Think of dancers in a circle holding hands. When they rotate around the center, everyone stays the same distance from the center.
Turning a steering wheel 30° then 40° is the same as turning it 70° once - angles add up directly.
